sections.paragraph:
m.-

(1) a wheel, Isa 28:28.

(2) with art. הַגִּלְגָּל (“a circle,” or according to Jos 5:9, “a rolling away”), Gilgal, pr.n. [a] of a town situated between Jericho and the Jordan ( Jos 4:19, 20 Jos 4:20 9:6 10:6 , Jos 10:7 14:6 15:7 ), where Samuel and Saul sacrificed ( 1Sa 10:8 11:14 , 15 1Sa 11:15 -13:49 ; 15:21 , 33 1Sa 15:33), and where prophets dwelt, 2Ki 4:38, but where also the worship of idols was practised ( Jud. 3:19 Hos 4:15 9:15 Amo 5:5); more fully בֵּית הַגִּלְגָּל Neh 12:29 Γάλγαλα, 1Ma_9:2 . The village mentioned, Jos 12:23 Deu 11:30, does not appear to have been different.

[“(b) a place or region near the western coast of Palestine, Jos 12:23.”]
